name: Heavy Ball
equations: x[k+1] = x[k] - alpha * grad_f(x[k]) + beta * (x[k] - x[k-1])

name: Nesterov's Accelerated Method
Triple Momentum Method
equations: x[k+1] = x[k] - alpha * grad_f(y[k]) + beta * (x[k] - x[k-1])

name: Quasi-Hyperbolic Momentum
equations: x[k+1] = x[k] - alpha * ((1 - nu) * grad_f(x[k]) + nu * g[k+1])

name: Stochastic Unified Momentum
bibtex:

name: Optimistic Mirror Descent
equations: x[k+1] = x[k] - 2 * eta * grad_f(x[k]) + eta * grad_f(x[k-1])

name: Douglas-Rachford Splitting
notes: Parameter alpha is the relaxation (alpha=1 is the standard form).

name: ADMM
notes: Parameter rho is the penalty parameter in the augmented Lagrangian.

name: Peaceman-Rachford Splitting
Chambolle-Pock
notes: Shown for the case where the linear operator is the identity (L = I). Parameters sigma and tau are the primal and dual step sizes; theta is the relaxation parameter.
equations: x2[k+1] = prox_gstar(x2[k] + sigma * ((1 + theta) * x1[k+1] - theta * x1[k]))

name: Loris-Verhoeven
equations: z[k+1] = prox_gstar( (1 - sigma * tau) * z[k] + sigma * (x[k+1] - tau * grad_h(x[k])) )

name: Davis-Yin Splitting
notes: Minimizes f(x) + g(x) + h(x) where h is smooth (grad_h oracle) and f, g have proximal operators (prox_f, prox_g). Parameter gamma is the proximal step size; t is the relaxation parameter.

name: PD3O
notes: Minimizes f(x) + g(Ax) + h(x) where f, g have proximal operators and h is smooth.
equations: s[k+1] = prox_gstar( (1 - tau * sigma) * s[k] + sigma * (2 * x[k] - z[k] - tau * grad_h(x[k])) )
z[k+1] = x[k] - tau * grad_h(x[k]) - tau * s[k+1]

name: Condat-Vu
equations: z[k+1] = prox_gstar( z[k] + sigma * (2 * x[k+1] - x[k]) )

name: Dual Condat-Vu
equations: x[k+1] = prox_f( x[k] - tau * (2 * z[k+1] - z[k] + grad_h(x[k])) )

name: Primal-Dual Davis-Yin
equations: x[k+1] = prox_f( x[k] - tau * (2 * z[k+1] - z[k]) - tau * grad_h(x[k] + tau * (z[k] - z[k+1])) )

name: Projected Reflected Gradient
equations: x[k+1] = P( x[k] - eta * grad_f(2 * x[k] - x[k-1]) )

name: Modified Arrow-Hurwicz
Extrapolation from the Past
Extragradient (Korpelevich)
Extragradient (Tseng)
Standard Average Consensus
Heavy-Ball Consensus
equations: x[k+1] = (1 + beta) * W * x[k] - beta * x[k-1]

name: Distributed Gradient Descent
notes: Fundamental distributed optimization algorithm. Does not achieve exact convergence due to the consensus-optimization trade-off.

name: NIDS
notes: Decentralized algorithm. The transfer function characterizes behavior per spectral mode of the graph Laplacian L (eigenvalue λ).
equations: x[k+1] = 2 * W * x[k] - W * x[k-1] - W * alpha * grad_f(x[k]) + W * alpha * grad_f(x[k-1])

name: Exact Diffusion
notes: Decentralized algorithm using adapt-correct-combine steps. The transfer function characterizes behavior per spectral mode of the graph Laplacian L (eigenvalue λ). Oracle-equivalent to NIDS.

name: EXTRA
notes: Decentralized algorithm using two gossip matrices W and W̃ = (I+W)/2. Achieves exact convergence.
equations: x[k+1] = (I + W) * x[k] - (W + I)/2 * x[k-1] - alpha * grad_f(x[k]) + alpha * grad_f(x[k-1])

name: DIGing
notes: Gradient tracking algorithm for decentralized optimization. Uses W̃ = W.

name: AugDGM
notes: Gradient tracking with the mixing matrix applied jointly to the state and gradient step.
equations: x[k+1] = W * x[k] - alpha * W * s[k]

name: SVL
notes: General distributed algorithm template with four tunable parameters. Achieves optimal worst-case convergence rate for time-varying graphs.
equations: x[k+1] = x[k] + beta * w[k] - alpha * grad_f(y[k]) - gamma * v[k]
transferFunction: (alpha*delta*lam*z - alpha*delta*lam - alpha*z + alpha)/(beta*lam + gamma*lam*z - gamma*lam + z**2 - 2*z + 1)

name: Unified EXTRA
notes: Generalization of EXTRA with overrelaxation parameter mu and function parameter l. Reduces to EXTRA when mu=1/2, l=1/alpha.
equations: x2[k+1] = x2[k] - mu*L*(-l*x1[k] + x2[k] + grad_f(x1[k]) + l*mu*L*x1[k])

name: Unified DIGing
notes: Generalization of DIGing with overrelaxation parameter mu and function parameter l. Reduces to DIGing when mu=1, l=0.
